_info: Knowledge base mapping YOLO-detected engine parts to faults, service checks, and guidance
_model_classes: 50 classes from fine-tuned YOLOv8 engine parts model

AIR COMPRESSOR.name: Air Compressor
AIR COMPRESSOR.description: Generates compressed air for pneumatic braking systems and accessories
AIR COMPRESSOR.location: Mounted to the engine block, driven by gear or belt

faults.name: Oil Leak
faults.visual: Oil residue or wet spots around compressor housing
faults.action: Check seals and gaskets, replace if leaking

faults.name: Excessive Noise
faults.visual: Rattling or knocking when running
faults.action: Check mounting bolts and internal bearings

faults.name: Low Air Pressure
faults.visual: Air pressure gauge reads below normal
faults.action: Check for air leaks in lines, inspect compressor valves

AIR COMPRESSOR.safety: Ensure engine is off before inspecting. Compressed air system is under pressure.

ALTERNATOR.name: Alternator
ALTERNATOR.description: Converts mechanical energy to electrical energy to charge the battery and power electrical systems while the engine runs
ALTERNATOR.location: Front of engine, driven by the serpentine/drive belt

faults.name: Belt Wear
faults.visual: Cracked, glazed, or fraying belt surface
faults.action: Inspect belt tension and condition, replace if worn

faults.name: Bearing Noise
faults.visual: Whining or grinding noise from alternator
faults.action: Replace alternator bearings or alternator unit

faults.name: Charging Failure
faults.visual: Battery warning light on dashboard
faults.action: Test output voltage (should be 13.5-14.5V), check wiring connections

faults.name: Loose Mount
faults.visual: Alternator wobbles or vibrates excessively
faults.action: Tighten mounting bolts to specification

ALTERNATOR.safety: Disconnect battery before removal. Belt can cause injury if engine is running.

BATTERY.name: Battery
BATTERY.description: Stores electrical energy to start the engine and power accessories when the engine is off
BATTERY.location: Engine bay, usually mounted in a battery box on one side

faults.name: Terminal Corrosion
faults.visual: White, green, or blue powder buildup on terminals
faults.action: Clean terminals with wire brush, apply terminal grease

faults.name: Swollen Case
faults.visual: Battery case appears bloated or expanded
faults.action: Replace battery immediately - indicates internal failure

faults.name: Low Electrolyte
faults.visual: Fluid level below plates visible through casing
faults.action: Top up with distilled water to correct level

faults.name: Loose Hold-Down
faults.visual: Battery moves or shifts in its tray
faults.action: Secure battery clamp and check tray condition

faults.name: Cable Damage
faults.visual: Frayed, cracked, or corroded battery cables
faults.action: Replace damaged cables, ensure tight connections

BATTERY.safety: Disconnect negative terminal first. Avoid sparks near battery (hydrogen gas risk). Wear eye protection.

BRAKE CALIPER.name: Brake Caliper
BRAKE CALIPER.description: Hydraulic clamp that squeezes brake pads against the rotor to slow the vehicle
BRAKE CALIPER.location: Behind each wheel, mounted to the steering knuckle or axle

faults.name: Fluid Leak
faults.visual: Wet brake fluid around caliper body or bleed nipple
faults.action: Replace caliper seals or caliper unit. Do NOT drive with brake fluid leaks.

faults.name: Sticking Piston
faults.visual: Uneven pad wear, vehicle pulls to one side under braking
faults.action: Rebuild or replace caliper, clean slide pins

faults.name: Torn Dust Boot
faults.visual: Ripped or missing rubber boot on caliper piston
faults.action: Replace dust boot to prevent contaminant entry

faults.name: Seized Slide Pins
faults.visual: Caliper does not float freely, uneven pad wear
faults.action: Remove, clean, and re-grease slide pins

BRAKE CALIPER.safety: Support vehicle on jack stands. Never rely on a jack alone. Brake dust may contain harmful particles.

BRAKE PAD.name: Brake Pad
BRAKE PAD.description: Friction material that presses against the brake rotor to create stopping force
BRAKE PAD.location: Inside the brake caliper, one pad on each side of the rotor

faults.name: Worn Below Minimum
faults.visual: Pad material less than 3mm thick
faults.action: Replace brake pads immediately. Measure with vernier calipers.

faults.name: Uneven Wear
faults.visual: One pad significantly thinner than the other
faults.action: Check caliper slide pins and piston, may need caliper service

faults.name: Glazed Surface
faults.visual: Smooth, shiny surface on pad friction material
faults.action: Replace pads, inspect rotor surface

faults.name: Cracking
faults.visual: Visible cracks in pad material
faults.action: Replace pads immediately

BRAKE PAD.safety: Use vernier calipers to measure lining thickness. Record and report measurements.

BRAKE ROTOR.name: Brake Rotor
BRAKE ROTOR.description: Metal disc that rotates with the wheel; brake pads clamp against it to create friction for stopping
BRAKE ROTOR.location: Behind each wheel, mounted to the wheel hub

faults.name: Scoring
faults.visual: Deep grooves or scratches on rotor surface
faults.action: Measure depth of scoring. Machine or replace rotor if beyond specification.

faults.name: Warping
faults.visual: Pulsation felt through brake pedal during braking
faults.action: Measure rotor runout with dial indicator. Machine or replace.

faults.name: Rust Buildup
faults.visual: Heavy rust on rotor surface or edges
faults.action: Light surface rust is normal. Heavy buildup indicates lack of use.

faults.name: Below Minimum Thickness
faults.visual: Rotor appears thin, check with micrometer
faults.action: Replace rotor. Minimum thickness is stamped on rotor edge.

BRAKE ROTOR.safety: Rotor edges can be sharp. Wear gloves when handling.

CAMSHAFT.name: Camshaft
CAMSHAFT.description: Rotating shaft with lobes that open and close the engine valves in the correct sequence and timing
CAMSHAFT.location: Inside the cylinder head (OHC) or engine block (pushrod engines)

faults.name: Lobe Wear
faults.visual: Flat spots on cam lobes, loss of engine power
faults.action: Requires engine disassembly. Replace camshaft and lifters.

faults.name: Timing Issue
faults.visual: Engine runs rough, misfires, check engine light
faults.action: Check timing chain/belt tension and alignment

faults.name: Bearing Wear
faults.visual: Ticking or tapping noise from top of engine
faults.action: Check oil pressure, inspect cam bearings

CAMSHAFT.safety: Major engine component. Requires professional service for internal repairs.

CARBERATOR.name: Carburetor
CARBERATOR.description: Mixes air and fuel in the correct ratio for combustion (older engines, pre-fuel injection)
CARBERATOR.location: Top of engine, mounted on the intake manifold

faults.name: Fuel Leak
faults.visual: Fuel dripping or wet spots around carburetor body
faults.action: Check float level, needle valve, and gaskets. Fire hazard.

faults.name: Rich Running
faults.visual: Black exhaust smoke, fuel smell, fouled spark plugs
faults.action: Adjust mixture screws, check choke operation

faults.name: Lean Running
faults.visual: Engine hesitates, backfires, overheats
faults.action: Check for vacuum leaks, adjust mixture, clean jets

faults.name: Sticking Choke
faults.visual: Hard cold start or runs rough when cold
faults.action: Clean and lubricate choke mechanism

CARBERATOR.safety: Fuel is highly flammable. No open flames. Keep fire extinguisher nearby.

CLUTCH PLATE.name: Clutch Plate
CLUTCH PLATE.description: Friction disc that engages and disengages power transfer between engine and transmission
CLUTCH PLATE.location: Between engine flywheel and transmission input shaft

faults.name: Worn Friction Material
faults.visual: Clutch slips under load, burning smell
faults.action: Replace clutch kit (disc, pressure plate, release bearing)

faults.name: Oil Contamination
faults.visual: Clutch grabs or chatters, oil on friction surface
faults.action: Find and fix oil leak source, replace clutch

faults.name: Warped Disc
faults.visual: Clutch judder on engagement
faults.action: Replace clutch disc

CLUTCH PLATE.safety: Requires transmission removal. Heavy components - use transmission jack.

COIL SPRING.name: Coil Spring
COIL SPRING.description: Helical metal spring that absorbs road impacts and supports vehicle weight
COIL SPRING.location: Between the frame/body and the axle or suspension arm at each wheel

faults.name: Broken Coil
faults.visual: Vehicle sits low on one corner, visible gap in spring
faults.action: Replace spring immediately. Broken springs can puncture tyres.

faults.name: Sagging
faults.visual: Vehicle rides lower than normal, uneven ride height
faults.action: Replace spring pair (both sides of same axle)

faults.name: Corrosion
faults.visual: Heavy rust or pitting on spring surface
faults.action: Inspect for weakening, replace if significantly corroded

COIL SPRING.safety: Springs are under extreme tension. Use proper spring compressor tool. Never use makeshift tools.

CRANKSHAFT.name: Crankshaft
CRANKSHAFT.description: Converts the reciprocating motion of pistons into rotational motion to drive the transmission
CRANKSHAFT.location: Bottom of the engine block, inside the crankcase

faults.name: Bearing Knock
faults.visual: Deep knocking noise from lower engine, especially under load
faults.action: Engine rebuild required. Check oil pressure immediately.

faults.name: Oil Seal Leak
faults.visual: Oil leak at front or rear of crankshaft
faults.action: Replace front or rear crankshaft seal

faults.name: Harmonic Balancer Failure
faults.visual: Wobbling pulley at front of crankshaft, vibration
faults.action: Replace harmonic balancer/damper

CRANKSHAFT.safety: Major internal engine component. Requires professional diagnosis and repair.

CYLINDER HEAD.name: Cylinder Head
CYLINDER HEAD.description: Caps the top of the cylinders, contains valves, spark plugs, and combustion chambers

faults.name: Head Gasket Failure
faults.visual: White exhaust smoke, coolant loss, milky oil
faults.action: Replace head gasket. Check head for warping before reassembly.

faults.name: Cracked Head
faults.visual: Coolant in oil or oil in coolant, overheating
faults.action: Pressure test the head. Replace if cracked.

faults.name: Warped Surface
faults.visual: Persistent coolant leak after gasket replacement
faults.action: Machine the head surface flat or replace

CYLINDER HEAD.safety: Allow engine to cool completely before any work. Coolant system is pressurized when hot.

DISTRIBUTOR.name: Distributor
DISTRIBUTOR.description: Routes high-voltage spark to each spark plug in the correct firing order (older ignition systems)
DISTRIBUTOR.location: Top of engine, driven by the camshaft

faults.name: Cap Cracks
faults.visual: Cracks, carbon tracking, or corrosion on distributor cap
faults.action: Replace distributor cap and rotor

faults.name: Worn Rotor
faults.visual: Pitting or burn marks on rotor contact tip
faults.action: Replace rotor with distributor cap

faults.name: Timing Drift
faults.visual: Engine runs rough, poor fuel economy
faults.action: Check and adjust ignition timing with timing light

DISTRIBUTOR.safety: High voltage system. Do not touch ignition wires while engine is running.

ENGINE BLOCK.name: Engine Block
ENGINE BLOCK.description: Main structural casting of the engine containing cylinders, coolant passages, and oil galleries

faults.name: Oil Leak
faults.visual: Oil seepage on block surface, drips underneath
faults.action: Identify leak source: gaskets, seals, or crack. Clean and monitor.

faults.name: Coolant Leak
faults.visual: Green/orange fluid on block, sweet smell
faults.action: Pressure test cooling system, check freeze plugs and gaskets

faults.name: Cracked Block
faults.visual: Coolant mixing with oil (milky dipstick), external crack visible
faults.action: Engine replacement typically required

faults.name: Loose Mounts
faults.visual: Excessive engine movement, vibration at idle
faults.action: Check and torque engine mounting bolts

ENGINE BLOCK.safety: Major component. Hot surfaces after running. Allow cooling before inspection.

ENGINE VALVE.name: Engine Valve
ENGINE VALVE.description: Opens and closes to control air/fuel intake and exhaust gas exit from each cylinder
ENGINE VALVE.location: Inside the cylinder head, one intake and one exhaust per cylinder minimum

faults.name: Burnt Valve
faults.visual: Persistent misfire on one cylinder, low compression
faults.action: Requires cylinder head removal and valve replacement

faults.name: Valve Seat Recession
faults.visual: Loss of power, rough idle
faults.action: Machine valve seats or replace cylinder head

faults.name: Worn Stem Seals
faults.visual: Blue smoke on startup, oil consumption
faults.action: Replace valve stem seals

ENGINE VALVE.safety: Internal engine component. Requires head removal for service.

FUEL INJECTOR.name: Fuel Injector
FUEL INJECTOR.description: Electronically controlled nozzle that sprays precise amounts of fuel into each cylinder
FUEL INJECTOR.location: Mounted in the intake manifold or directly in the cylinder head

faults.name: Clogged Nozzle
faults.visual: Rough idle, misfire, poor fuel economy
faults.action: Use fuel injector cleaner or have professionally cleaned

faults.name: Fuel Leak
faults.visual: Fuel smell, wet spots around injector O-rings
faults.action: Replace injector O-rings or injector. Fire hazard.

faults.name: Electrical Fault
faults.visual: Injector not clicking, check engine light
faults.action: Check wiring connector and injector resistance

FUEL INJECTOR.safety: Fuel under pressure. Depressurize fuel system before removal. No open flames.

FUSE BOX.name: Fuse Box
FUSE BOX.description: Central distribution panel containing fuses that protect electrical circuits from overload
FUSE BOX.location: Engine bay (main fuse box) and under dashboard (cabin fuse box)

faults.name: Blown Fuse
faults.visual: Broken fuse element visible through clear casing
faults.action: Replace with same amperage fuse. Investigate cause if it blows repeatedly.

faults.name: Corrosion
faults.visual: Green/white deposits on fuse contacts
faults.action: Clean contacts with electrical cleaner, apply dielectric grease

faults.name: Melted Connector
faults.visual: Discolored or melted plastic around fuse slot
faults.action: Indicates short circuit. Professional electrical diagnosis required.

FUSE BOX.safety: Disconnect battery before working on electrical systems.

GAS CAP.name: Fuel Cap
GAS CAP.description: Seals the fuel tank filler neck, maintains fuel system pressure and prevents evaporative emissions
GAS CAP.location: On the side of the vehicle at the fuel filler neck

faults.name: Damaged Seal
faults.visual: Cracked or missing rubber gasket on cap
faults.action: Replace fuel cap

faults.name: Check Engine Light
faults.visual: Evaporative emissions code triggered
faults.action: Ensure cap clicks tight. Replace if seal is worn.

faults.name: Missing Cap
faults.visual: Fuel filler neck is open/uncovered
faults.action: Replace fuel cap to prevent contamination and emissions

GAS CAP.safety: No special precautions. Keep away from open flames.

HEADLIGHTS.name: Headlights
HEADLIGHTS.description: Forward-facing vehicle lights for illumination and visibility
HEADLIGHTS.location: Front of vehicle, mounted in the front body panel

faults.name: Burnt Bulb
faults.visual: One or both headlights not illuminating
faults.action: Replace headlight bulb. Check for matching wattage.

faults.name: Cloudy Lens
faults.visual: Yellowed, hazy, or foggy headlight lens
faults.action: Polish with headlight restoration kit or replace lens

faults.name: Misaligned Beam
faults.visual: Light pattern too high, too low, or off-center
faults.action: Adjust headlight aim using adjustment screws

faults.name: Cracked Lens
faults.visual: Visible crack or chip in headlight cover
faults.action: Replace headlight assembly to prevent moisture ingress

HEADLIGHTS.safety: Some bulbs are halogen/HID - do not touch glass with bare fingers (oil causes hotspots).

IDLER ARM.name: Idler Arm
IDLER ARM.description: Supports the steering linkage on the passenger side, mirroring the pitman arm on the driver side
IDLER ARM.location: Passenger side of the frame, connecting the centre link to the frame

faults.name: Excessive Play
faults.visual: Looseness when rocking the steering, wandering on road
faults.action: Replace idler arm. Check all steering linkage while servicing.

faults.name: Worn Bushings
faults.visual: Clunking noise over bumps, loose steering feel
faults.action: Replace idler arm assembly

faults.name: Grease Loss
faults.visual: Dry or cracked dust boot, no grease visible
faults.action: Lubricate or replace if boot is torn

IDLER ARM.safety: Steering component - failure can cause loss of vehicle control. Inspect thoroughly.

IGNITION COIL.name: Ignition Coil
IGNITION COIL.description: Transforms battery voltage (12V) to the high voltage needed to fire spark plugs (20,000-40,000V)
IGNITION COIL.location: Top of engine, one per cylinder (coil-on-plug) or single unit with wires

faults.name: Coil Failure
faults.visual: Engine misfire, rough idle, check engine light
faults.action: Test coil resistance with multimeter. Replace if out of specification.

faults.name: Cracked Housing
faults.visual: Visible crack in coil body, arcing in dark
faults.action: Replace ignition coil immediately

faults.name: Connector Corrosion
faults.visual: Green deposits on electrical connector
faults.action: Clean connector, apply dielectric grease

IGNITION COIL.safety: High voltage component. Do not touch while engine is running.

INSTRUMENT CLUSTER.name: Instrument Cluster
INSTRUMENT CLUSTER.description: Dashboard panel displaying vehicle speed, RPM, fuel level, temperature, and warning indicators
INSTRUMENT CLUSTER.location: Behind the steering wheel on the dashboard

faults.name: Dead Gauges
faults.visual: One or more gauges not responding
faults.action: Check fuses, wiring connectors, and sending units

faults.name: Warning Lights
faults.visual: Warning lights illuminated on dashboard
faults.action: Diagnose with DXII scan tool, address fault codes

faults.name: Backlight Failure
faults.visual: Cluster not illuminated at night
faults.action: Replace bulbs in instrument cluster

INSTRUMENT CLUSTER.safety: Disconnect battery before removing instrument cluster.

LEAF SPRING.name: Leaf Spring
LEAF SPRING.description: Layered steel spring that supports vehicle weight and absorbs road impacts, common on truck axles
LEAF SPRING.location: Between the frame and axle, running lengthwise under the vehicle

faults.name: Broken Leaf
faults.visual: Visible crack or separation in spring leaf
faults.action: Replace leaf spring assembly. Vehicle may sit unevenly.

faults.name: Sagging
faults.visual: Vehicle rides lower on one side, uneven load capacity
faults.action: Replace leaf spring pack

faults.name: Loose U-Bolts
faults.visual: Spring shifting on axle, clunking noise
faults.action: Torque U-bolts to specification (195 Nm)

faults.name: Worn Bushings
faults.visual: Squeaking, movement at spring eyes
faults.action: Replace spring eye bushings

LEAF SPRING.safety: Springs are under load. Use proper support when servicing.

LOWER CONTROL ARM.name: Lower Control Arm
LOWER CONTROL ARM.description: Connects the wheel hub/knuckle to the vehicle frame, allowing controlled vertical wheel movement
LOWER CONTROL ARM.location: Lower front suspension, between the frame and steering knuckle

faults.name: Worn Ball Joint
faults.visual: Clunking over bumps, play in steering
faults.action: Replace ball joint or control arm assembly. Safety critical.

faults.name: Torn Bushing
faults.visual: Cracked or separated rubber bushing
faults.action: Replace control arm bushings or full arm

faults.name: Bent Arm
faults.visual: Vehicle pulls to one side, uneven tyre wear
faults.action: Replace control arm. Check alignment after replacement.

LOWER CONTROL ARM.safety: Suspension component under load. Support vehicle properly. Ball joint failure can cause wheel separation.

MUFFLER.name: Muffler
MUFFLER.description: Reduces engine exhaust noise using internal baffles and chambers
MUFFLER.location: Underneath the vehicle, part of the exhaust system between catalytic converter and tailpipe

faults.name: Rust Through
faults.visual: Holes or flaking rust on muffler body
faults.action: Replace muffler. Check exhaust hangers.

faults.name: Exhaust Leak
faults.visual: Loud exhaust noise, soot marks at joints
faults.action: Repair or replace leaking section, check clamps

faults.name: Rattling
faults.visual: Internal baffles loose, rattling noise at idle
faults.action: Replace muffler

faults.name: Loose Hangers
faults.visual: Muffler hanging low or contacting other parts
faults.action: Replace exhaust hangers/rubber mounts

MUFFLER.safety: Exhaust system is extremely hot after running. Allow full cool-down before touching.

OIL FILTER.name: Oil Filter
OIL FILTER.description: Filters contaminants from engine oil to protect internal engine components from wear
OIL FILTER.location: Side or bottom of engine block, accessible from underneath

faults.name: Leaking Gasket
faults.visual: Oil drip or seepage around filter base
faults.action: Ensure old gasket was removed. Reinstall with new gasket lightly oiled.

faults.name: Clogged Filter
faults.visual: Low oil pressure, oil pressure warning light
faults.action: Replace oil filter immediately

faults.name: Over-Tightened
faults.visual: Crushed gasket, difficult to remove
faults.action: Hand tighten plus 3/4 turn only. Use filter strap wrench for removal.

OIL FILTER.safety: Hot oil can cause burns. Let engine cool before changing. Use drain pan.

OIL PAN.name: Oil Pan
OIL PAN.description: Reservoir at the bottom of the engine that collects and stores engine oil
OIL PAN.location: Very bottom of the engine block

faults.name: Drain Plug Leak
faults.visual: Oil drip from drain plug area
faults.action: Check drain plug torque (41 Nm). Replace crush washer.

faults.name: Gasket Leak
faults.visual: Oil seepage along pan-to-block mating surface
faults.action: Replace oil pan gasket

faults.name: Impact Damage
faults.visual: Dent or crack from road debris impact
faults.action: Replace oil pan if cracked. Monitor oil level closely.

faults.name: Rust
faults.visual: Corrosion on oil pan surface
faults.action: Monitor for perforation. Clean and protect with paint.

OIL PAN.safety: Hot oil can burn. Allow engine to cool. Use proper drain pan and dispose of oil responsibly.

OIL PRESSURE SENSOR.name: Oil Pressure Sensor
OIL PRESSURE SENSOR.description: Monitors engine oil pressure and sends signal to the dashboard gauge or warning light
OIL PRESSURE SENSOR.location: On the engine block, usually near the oil filter

faults.name: False Reading
faults.visual: Oil pressure gauge reads zero or erratic, but engine sounds normal
faults.action: Replace sensor. Verify with manual gauge if in doubt.

faults.name: Oil Leak
faults.visual: Oil seepage around sensor threads
faults.action: Apply thread sealant and retighten, or replace sensor

faults.name: Wiring Issue
faults.visual: Intermittent gauge reading, loose connector
faults.action: Check and clean electrical connector

OIL PRESSURE SENSOR.safety: If oil pressure warning light comes on while driving, stop engine immediately.

OVERFLOW TANK.name: Coolant Overflow Tank
OVERFLOW TANK.description: Reservoir that stores excess coolant as the engine heats up and returns it as it cools down
OVERFLOW TANK.location: Engine bay, usually on one side near the radiator, translucent plastic tank

faults.name: Low Level
faults.visual: Coolant below minimum mark on tank
faults.action: Top up coolant to correct level. Check for leaks if level drops repeatedly.

faults.name: Cracked Tank
faults.visual: Visible crack or coolant stain on tank body
faults.action: Replace overflow tank

faults.name: Discolored Coolant
faults.visual: Coolant is brown, rusty, or contaminated
faults.action: Flush cooling system and refill with correct coolant

faults.name: Missing Cap
faults.visual: Cap missing or not sealing properly
faults.action: Replace cap to maintain system pressure

OVERFLOW TANK.safety: NEVER open when engine is hot. Pressurized system can cause severe burns.

OXYGEN SENSOR.name: Oxygen Sensor
OXYGEN SENSOR.description: Monitors oxygen content in exhaust gases to help the ECU adjust the air-fuel mixture
OXYGEN SENSOR.location: Threaded into the exhaust pipe, before and/or after the catalytic converter

faults.name: Contaminated
faults.visual: Check engine light, poor fuel economy, rough idle
faults.action: Replace oxygen sensor. Use correct part for pre/post-cat position.

faults.name: Wiring Damage
faults.visual: Frayed or melted wires near exhaust heat
faults.action: Repair wiring or replace sensor with pigtail

faults.name: Slow Response
faults.visual: Sluggish throttle response, increased emissions
faults.action: Replace sensor

OXYGEN SENSOR.safety: Exhaust components are extremely hot. Allow full cool-down.

PISTON.name: Piston
PISTON.description: Cylindrical component that moves up and down inside the cylinder, compressing air-fuel mixture and transmitting force to the crankshaft
PISTON.location: Inside each cylinder of the engine block

faults.name: Ring Wear
faults.visual: Blue exhaust smoke, high oil consumption
faults.action: Engine rebuild with new piston rings. Check cylinder bore.

faults.name: Piston Slap
faults.visual: Knocking noise from engine when cold
faults.action: Monitor condition. May require rebore and new pistons.

faults.name: Cracked Piston
faults.visual: Loss of compression, rough running
faults.action: Engine rebuild required

PISTON.safety: Internal engine component. Requires complete engine disassembly.

PRESSURE PLATE.name: Pressure Plate
PRESSURE PLATE.description: Spring-loaded plate that clamps the clutch disc against the flywheel to transmit engine torque
PRESSURE PLATE.location: Bolted to the flywheel, between the flywheel and transmission

faults.name: Worn Fingers
faults.visual: Clutch does not fully engage or release, hard gear changes
faults.action: Replace clutch kit (pressure plate, disc, bearing)

faults.name: Hot Spots
faults.visual: Blue discoloration on pressure plate surface
faults.action: Resurface or replace. Indicates clutch slipping/overheating.

faults.name: Broken Spring
faults.visual: Clutch judder, uneven engagement
faults.action: Replace pressure plate

PRESSURE PLATE.safety: Requires transmission removal. Use transmission jack.

RADIATOR.name: Radiator
RADIATOR.description: Heat exchanger that cools engine coolant by transferring heat to the air flowing through its fins
RADIATOR.location: Front of engine bay, directly behind the grille

faults.name: Leaking
faults.visual: Green/orange coolant drips, wet spots on radiator
faults.action: Locate leak. Minor leaks may be patched, major leaks require replacement.

faults.name: Clogged Fins
faults.visual: Debris, bugs, or dirt blocking airflow through fins
faults.action: Clean with compressed air or gentle water spray from engine side

faults.name: Internal Blockage
faults.visual: Engine overheating despite full coolant, uneven heat across radiator
faults.action: Flush radiator or replace

faults.name: Cracked Plastic Tanks
faults.visual: Coolant leak at plastic-to-aluminium joints
faults.action: Replace radiator

RADIATOR.safety: NEVER open radiator cap when engine is hot. Allow complete cool-down first.

RADIATOR FAN.name: Radiator Fan
RADIATOR FAN.description: Draws air through the radiator to cool the engine, especially at low speeds or idle
RADIATOR FAN.location: Attached to the radiator or driven by the engine, behind the radiator

faults.name: Cracked Blade
faults.visual: Visible crack, chip, or missing piece on fan blade
faults.action: Replace fan immediately. Broken blade can cause serious damage.

faults.name: Not Engaging
faults.visual: Fan does not spin when engine is hot
faults.action: Check fan clutch, relay, fuse, or temperature sensor

faults.name: Loose Mount
faults.visual: Fan wobbles, scraping noise against shroud
faults.action: Tighten mounting bolts, check fan clutch

faults.name: Shroud Damage
faults.visual: Cracked or broken fan shroud
faults.action: Replace shroud to maintain proper airflow

RADIATOR FAN.safety: Fan can start unexpectedly with engine off on some vehicles. Keep hands clear.

RADIATOR HOSE.name: Radiator Hose
RADIATOR HOSE.description: Rubber hose connecting the engine to the radiator, carrying hot coolant to be cooled and returned
RADIATOR HOSE.location: Upper hose from engine top to radiator top; lower hose from radiator bottom to water pump

faults.name: Bulging
faults.visual: Hose appears swollen or ballooned at one point
faults.action: Replace hose immediately. Indicates weakening that could burst.

faults.name: Cracking
faults.visual: Surface cracks, especially near clamp areas
faults.action: Replace hose before it fails

faults.name: Soft/Spongy
faults.visual: Hose collapses easily when squeezed
faults.action: Replace hose - internal degradation

faults.name: Leaking at Clamp
faults.visual: Coolant drip or stain at hose clamp connection
faults.action: Tighten clamp or replace. Check for hose neck corrosion.

RADIATOR HOSE.safety: Hot coolant under pressure. Never open or disconnect when engine is hot.

RADIO.name: Radio/Stereo

faults.name: No Power
faults.visual: Radio screen blank, no sound
faults.action: Check fuse, wiring connector behind unit

faults.name: No Sound
faults.visual: Display works but no audio output
faults.action: Check speaker wiring, amplifier fuse

faults.name: Static/Interference
faults.visual: Poor reception, crackling sounds
faults.action: Check antenna connection, suppress ignition interference

RADIO.safety: Disconnect battery before removing head unit.

RIM.name: Wheel Rim
RIM.description: Metal wheel that the tyre is mounted on, connects to the hub via wheel studs
RIM.location: At each wheel position on the vehicle

faults.name: Bent Rim
faults.visual: Vibration at speed, visible wobble when spinning
faults.action: Replace rim. Check tyre for damage as well.

faults.name: Cracked Rim
faults.visual: Visible crack in rim, slow tyre leak
faults.action: Replace rim immediately. Do not drive on a cracked rim.

faults.name: Corrosion
faults.visual: Flaking paint, rust, pitting on rim surface
faults.action: Clean and refinish, or replace if structural corrosion

faults.name: Loose Wheel Nuts
faults.visual: Wheel movement, clicking noise while driving
faults.action: Torque wheel nuts to specification (440-590 Nm)

RIM.safety: Support vehicle on jack stands before removing wheels. Torque nuts in star pattern.

SHIFT KNOB.name: Shift Knob
SHIFT KNOB.description: Handle on top of the gear shift lever used to change gears
SHIFT KNOB.location: Inside the cab, on the gear shift lever

faults.name: Loose Knob
faults.visual: Shift knob rotates freely or is wobbly
faults.action: Tighten or re-thread shift knob

faults.name: Worn Pattern
faults.visual: Gear position markings worn off
faults.action: Replace shift knob for clear gear identification

SIDE MIRROR.name: Side Mirror
SIDE MIRROR.description: Exterior mirror providing rear and side visibility for the driver
SIDE MIRROR.location: Mounted on both sides of the cab, attached to door or A-pillar

faults.name: Loose Mount
faults.visual: Mirror vibrates or moves while driving
faults.action: Tighten mounting bolts and check bracket condition

faults.name: Cracked Glass
faults.visual: Broken or cracked mirror glass
faults.action: Replace mirror glass or complete mirror assembly

faults.name: Adjustment Failure
faults.visual: Mirror cannot be adjusted to correct position
faults.action: Check adjustment mechanism, lubricate or replace

SIDE MIRROR.safety: Functional mirrors are a legal requirement. Replace damaged mirrors promptly.

SPARK PLUG.name: Spark Plug
SPARK PLUG.description: Creates the electrical spark that ignites the air-fuel mixture in each cylinder
SPARK PLUG.location: Screwed into the cylinder head, one per cylinder, accessible from the top of the engine

faults.name: Carbon Fouling
faults.visual: Black, sooty deposits on electrode and insulator
faults.action: Replace plug. Indicates rich mixture or oil burning.

faults.name: Oil Fouling
faults.visual: Wet, oily deposits on plug
faults.action: Replace plug. Investigate oil leak into cylinder (worn rings or valve seals).

faults.name: Worn Electrode
faults.visual: Rounded or eroded centre electrode, wide gap
faults.action: Replace spark plug. Check and set correct gap on new plug.

faults.name: Cracked Insulator
faults.visual: Visible crack in white ceramic insulator
faults.action: Replace immediately. Can cause misfire and engine damage.

SPARK PLUG.safety: Work on a cool engine. Remove one at a time to avoid mixing up wire order.

SPOILER.name: Spoiler
SPOILER.description: Aerodynamic body panel that affects airflow and stability at speed
SPOILER.location: Rear of vehicle, mounted on trunk lid or roof edge

faults.name: Loose Mount
faults.visual: Spoiler rattles or moves, mounting holes visible
faults.action: Tighten mounting hardware

faults.name: Cracked
faults.visual: Visible crack or break in spoiler body
faults.action: Replace or repair with appropriate adhesive

STARTER.name: Starter Motor
STARTER.description: Electric motor that cranks the engine flywheel to start the engine
STARTER.location: Lower side of engine, bolted to the transmission bellhousing, engaging the flywheel

faults.name: Clicking No Crank
faults.visual: Rapid clicking sound when turning key, engine does not crank
faults.action: Check battery voltage first. If battery OK, replace starter solenoid or motor.

faults.name: Grinding Noise
faults.visual: Metal grinding sound during cranking
faults.action: Check starter bendix gear and flywheel ring gear teeth

faults.name: Slow Cranking
faults.visual: Engine turns over slowly
faults.action: Check battery and cable connections first, then test starter draw

faults.name: Stuck Engaged
faults.visual: Starter continues running after engine starts
faults.action: Turn off engine immediately. Replace starter or solenoid.

STARTER.safety: Disconnect battery before working on starter to prevent accidental engagement.

TAILLIGHTS.name: Tail Lights
TAILLIGHTS.description: Rear vehicle lights including brake lights, indicators, and running lights
TAILLIGHTS.location: Rear of vehicle, mounted in the rear body panel

faults.name: Burnt Bulb
faults.visual: One or more rear lights not working
faults.action: Replace bulb. Check for matching wattage.

faults.name: Cracked Lens
faults.visual: Broken or cracked tail light lens
faults.action: Replace lens or assembly to prevent moisture damage

faults.name: Wiring Fault
faults.visual: Intermittent operation, lights flicker
faults.action: Check ground connection and wiring harness

TAILLIGHTS.safety: Functional brake lights are a legal requirement.

THERMOSTAT.name: Thermostat
THERMOSTAT.description: Temperature-sensitive valve that regulates coolant flow to maintain optimal engine operating temperature
THERMOSTAT.location: In the thermostat housing where the upper radiator hose meets the engine

faults.name: Stuck Open
faults.visual: Engine takes very long to warm up, heater blows cold
faults.action: Replace thermostat

faults.name: Stuck Closed
faults.visual: Engine overheats quickly, upper hose not getting hot
faults.action: Replace thermostat immediately to prevent engine damage

faults.name: Housing Leak
faults.visual: Coolant leak at thermostat housing
faults.action: Replace thermostat gasket, check housing for cracks

THERMOSTAT.safety: Allow engine to cool before removal. Coolant will drain when thermostat is removed.

TORQUE CONVERTER.name: Torque Converter
TORQUE CONVERTER.description: Fluid coupling between the engine and automatic transmission that multiplies torque and allows engine to idle while vehicle is stopped
TORQUE CONVERTER.location: Between the engine and automatic transmission, bolted to the flywheel/flexplate

faults.name: Shudder
faults.visual: Vibration during light acceleration, feels like driving on rumble strips
faults.action: May need transmission fluid flush or torque converter replacement

faults.name: Fluid Leak
faults.visual: Transmission fluid leak at front of transmission
faults.action: Replace torque converter seal

faults.name: Stall Speed Issue
faults.visual: Vehicle sluggish from stop, slipping sensation
faults.action: Test stall speed. Replace torque converter if out of specification.

TORQUE CONVERTER.safety: Requires transmission removal. Heavy component - use transmission jack.

TRANSMISSION.name: Transmission
TRANSMISSION.description: Gearbox that changes the gear ratio between the engine and drive wheels for different speeds and loads
TRANSMISSION.location: Bolted to the rear of the engine, extending under the vehicle

faults.name: Fluid Leak
faults.visual: Transmission fluid drip or wet area under vehicle
faults.action: Identify leak source: seals, gaskets, or cooler lines. Repair and top up fluid.

faults.name: Hard Shifting
faults.visual: Difficulty engaging gears, grinding noise
faults.action: Check fluid level and condition. May need linkage adjustment or synchro repair.

faults.name: Slipping
faults.visual: Engine revs rise without corresponding acceleration
faults.action: Check fluid level. May need transmission rebuild.

faults.name: Loose Mounts
faults.visual: Transmission vibrates, clunking when shifting
faults.action: Torque mounting bolts to specification (62 Nm)

TRANSMISSION.safety: Support transmission properly when working underneath. Hot fluid can burn.

VACUUM BRAKE BOOSTER.name: Vacuum Brake Booster
VACUUM BRAKE BOOSTER.description: Uses engine vacuum to multiply the force applied to the brake pedal for easier braking
VACUUM BRAKE BOOSTER.location: Between the brake pedal and the master cylinder, on the firewall in the engine bay

faults.name: Loss of Assist
faults.visual: Brake pedal is hard to press, requires excessive force
faults.action: Check vacuum hose for leaks or disconnection. Test booster diaphragm.

faults.name: Hissing Sound
faults.visual: Hissing noise when pressing brake pedal
faults.action: Check vacuum hose connections and booster diaphragm seal

faults.name: Brake Pedal Sinks
faults.visual: Pedal slowly sinks to floor when held steady
faults.action: Check master cylinder for internal leak. May also be booster failure.

VACUUM BRAKE BOOSTER.safety: Braking system is safety critical. Test thoroughly after any repair. Do not drive if brakes are not functioning properly.

VALVE LIFTER.name: Valve Lifter
VALVE LIFTER.description: Transfers camshaft motion to the valves, either directly (OHC) or via pushrods (OHV)
VALVE LIFTER.location: Inside the engine, between the camshaft and valve stems

faults.name: Ticking Noise
faults.visual: Rhythmic ticking or tapping from top of engine
faults.action: Check oil level first. May need lifter replacement or adjustment.

faults.name: Collapsed Lifter
faults.visual: Persistent tick, misfire on one cylinder
faults.action: Replace lifter. Check camshaft lobe for wear.

faults.name: Oil Starvation
faults.visual: Multiple lifters ticking, especially on cold start
faults.action: Check oil level and condition. Consider oil flush.

VALVE LIFTER.safety: Internal engine component. Requires valve cover removal at minimum.

WATER PUMP.name: Water Pump
WATER PUMP.description: Circulates coolant through the engine block, heater core, and radiator to maintain operating temperature
WATER PUMP.location: Front of engine, driven by the timing belt/chain or serpentine belt

faults.name: Bearing Noise
faults.visual: Whining or grinding from front of engine, play in pump shaft
faults.action: Replace water pump. If timing belt driven, replace belt at same time.

faults.name: Coolant Leak
faults.visual: Coolant drip from weep hole at bottom of pump
faults.action: Replace water pump. Weep hole leak means internal seal has failed.

faults.name: Impeller Failure
faults.visual: Engine overheats but pump appears to be turning
faults.action: Replace water pump - impeller may be corroded or slipping on shaft

WATER PUMP.safety: Allow engine to cool. Coolant system is pressurized when hot.

WINDOW REGULATOR.name: Window Regulator
WINDOW REGULATOR.description: Mechanism inside the door that raises and lowers the window glass

faults.name: Motor Failure
faults.visual: Window does not move, motor makes no sound
faults.action: Check fuse and wiring first. Replace motor if faulty.

faults.name: Slow Operation
faults.visual: Window moves slowly or struggles to go up
faults.action: Lubricate tracks and check motor. May need replacement.

faults.name: Glass Fell
faults.visual: Window glass dropped inside the door
faults.action: Replace window regulator mechanism

WINDOW REGULATOR.safety: Disconnect battery before working inside door panels to avoid short circuits.
